on

The Super Falcons of Nigeria will today (Friday) take on the Indomitable Lionesses of Cameroon in a crucial third round first-leg Paris 2024 Olympic qualifier.

The match will take place at the Stade de la Réunification in Douala, Cameroon, by 6 00 pm, Nigerian time.

The game will be broadcast live on the official YouTube channels of the Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) and the Cameroonian Football Federation (FECAFOOT).

Both teams are fighting for a coveted spot at the 2024 Olympic Games in Paris, France.

The winner of the two-legged clash will progress to the final qualifying round and will face the winner between South Africa and Tanzania.

Only two teams will represent Africa in the competition.

Historically, Nigeria have won nine out of the 11 encounters with Cameroon.

The Super Falcons defeated Cameroon 1-0 in their most recent tie at the 2022 WAFCON.

Cameroon’s two wins against Nigeria came on home soil.

Today’s match promises to be a fierce and tightly contested affair.

Meanwhile, Super Falcons players to watch out for include the likes of Ashleigh Plumptre, Asisat Oshoala, Osinachi Ohale and Toni Payne.

For Cameroon, Ndanza Fegue Collette, Gabrielle Onguene and Ngock Yango Grace are the players to watch out for.
